description_long: This town hall complex features a prominent main building with two symmetrical towers at the front, each topped with a spire and cross, suggesting a blend of civic and ecclesiastical architecture. The facade is richly detailed with arched windows, pilasters, and a central entrance framed by columns and decorative moldings. The roof is steeply pitched with multiple gables and dormers, and the structure is surrounded by a low fence, demarcating its grounds. Adjacent to the main hall are several attached and detached auxiliary buildings, each with simpler, gabled roofs and regular window patterns, likely serving administrative or community functions. The entire complex sits on a rectangular plot, with paved walkways and a clear separation between the main hall and the secondary buildings. The overall appearance is stately and formal, with a strong sense of symmetry and historic architectural detailing.
description: A stately town hall complex with twin-towered main building, ornate facade, and several adjoining auxiliary structures, all enclosed by a low fence. The architecture features steep roofs, arched windows, and decorative columns.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main entrance with twin towers, ornate facade, and the adjacent auxiliary buildings to the right.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the main hall and the clustered arrangement of the auxiliary buildings, with less facade ornamentation visible.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the length of the main hall, the twin towers, and the relationship between the main and auxiliary buildings.
description_view_3: Frontal view directly facing the main entrance, showcasing the symmetry of the towers, central doorway, and decorative elements.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Prominent columns, arched windows, pilasters, cornices, and decorative moldings on the main facade; minimal ornamentation on auxiliary buildings.
attribute_entrance_design: Main entrance features a central double door framed by columns and an arched portico, with steps leading up and decorative moldings above.
